description: Add security headers to all 7 Next.js app-router apps via their next.config files. All 7 apps already have error.tsx boundaries - this feature focuses on hardening the HTTP response headers.
steps: Update the next.config for all 7 Next.js apps to add security headers via the headers() async function. The apps and their config file formats: (1) apps/quantai/next.config.mjs - uses module.exports, (2) apps/quantchat/next.config.mjs - uses module.exports, (3) apps/quantdocs/next.config.mjs - uses export default, (4) apps/quantdrive/next.config.mjs - uses export default, (5) apps/quantmeet/next.config.mjs - uses export default, (6) apps/quantcalendar/next.config.mjs - uses export default, (7) apps/quantmail/next.config.mjs - uses export default.

Add these headers to all routes (source: '/:path*'): Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, X-DNS-Prefetch-Control: on, Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(). Note: quantmeet may need camera/microphone permissions - set those to camera=(self), microphone=(self) for quantmeet only.
Run pnpm typecheck && pnpm build && pnpm test to verify everything still passes.
acceptance_criteria: All 7 Next.js apps have security headers configured in next.config
Headers include HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy
findings: quantai and quantchat use next.config.js (not .mjs as noted in the task description) with module.exports. quantmail uses next.config.js with export default. quantdocs, quantdrive, quantmeet, quantcalendar use next.config.mjs with export default. All 111 typecheck, 88 build, and 114 test tasks pass. Prettier reformatted the .js files via lint-staged hook during commit.
